title: Marbella Catamaran Sunset Charter: Costs, Tips & Best Boats for 2026
html:
If you are searching for a catamaran charter at sunset in Marbella, the best departures leave from Puerto José Banús, with shared tours starting at around €80 per person and private catamarans from €1,200. BoatHire24 connects you directly with local operators offering skippered charters in Marbella.

Where to set off in Marbella

Puerto José Banús, the Marina Bajadilla, and Club del Mar are the primary departure points for sunset cruises on the Mediterranean Sea. Many skippers also pick up from Cabopino Marina, a quieter alternative to the west. The scenic sweep of the Costa del Sol includes the Bay of Marbella, the protected Cabo Pino dunes, and, on clear evenings, the distant profile of Gibraltar. For a longer voyage, the anchored motor yachts off the coast of Puerto Banús make a fine passage landmark. You can skippered charters in Marbella for your chosen dates.

Which boat to rent

Choose your vessel based on group size and the experience you want: from sleek motor yachts to spacious catamarans. All options come skippered unless you hold a licence. One option worth a look is the Sunseeker 68 Sport charter page.

  1. Motor yacht (e.g. Sunseeker 68 Sport), ideal for a stylish sunset dash, up to 10 guests, from €2,500 for three hours. See the Sunseeker 68 Sport charter page for full amenities.
  2. Sailing catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 46), stable and social, perfect for groups of 8, 12, from €1,600 for a half-day including crew and drinks.
  3. RIB or Axopar tender, fast access to hidden coves, max 6 guests, from €500 for two hours.
  4. Classic motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 50), an elegant choice for couples or small families, up to 8 guests, from €1,800 for a four-hour sunset cruise.
  5. Luxury crewed yacht (e.g. Maiora 80), full crew and open bar for larger groups, up to 14 guests, from €4,000 for a five-hour evening charter.

How much does it cost to charter a catamaran in Marbella?

A sunset catamaran charter in Marbella ranges from €80 per person on a shared tour to €4,000 for a private crewed yacht. Prices vary by vessel size, season, and extras.

  • Shared sunset catamaran (2 hours): €80, €120 per person
  • Private small motorboat (half-day, 4 guests): €500, €700
  • Private catamaran (full-day, 8, 12 guests): €1,200, €2,400
  • Crewed luxury yacht (evening, 10, 14 guests): €2,500, €4,000

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella?

No, you do not need a licence if you book a skippered charter; the operator provides a professional captain and handles all navigation. Private bareboat charters require an ICC (International Certificate of Competence) or equivalent, but most sunset trips in Marbella are skippered. The operator will confirm exact requirements at the time of booking. You can also see more on the BoatHire24 marketplace.

For the best sunset colours, aim to depart one hour before the official sunset time: the sky ablaze with orange and pink is a spectacle you will not forget. A light poncho can be useful as the sea breeze cools once the sun drops.

Season and sailing conditions

The high season runs from June to September, with July and August the busiest; the local Levante wind can bring afternoon chop, so skippers often wait for the evening calm. In 2026, May and October also offer pleasant evening sailing with fewer crowds. The Mediterranean Sea around Marbella remains warm, and typical sunset trips cover up to 10 nautical miles along the coast.

At a glance

  • Price band: €80 per person to €4,000 for a private yacht
  • Peak months: June to September (July and August busiest)
  • Trip length: 2 to 5 hours
  • Guest capacity: 4 to 14 depending on vessel
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

What time does a typical sunset charter depart?

Most sunset charters in Marbella depart between 18:00 and 19:30 in summer, adjusted to the daily sunset time.

Can I bring my own food and drinks?

Many operators allow you to bring your own catering, while inclusive packages often provide cava, soft drinks, and tapas.

Is it possible to swim during the cruise?

Yes, skippers usually stop at a calm cove near Cabopino or off the Marbella coast for a swim if guests wish.

What is the cancellation policy?

Policies vary by listing; check the terms on each boat’s page or contact the operator directly through the platform.

Whether you plan a romantic evening or a family celebration, a sunset catamaran charter in Marbella remains one of the most memorable ways to enjoy the Costa del Sol. You can compare verified boats on the BoatHire24 marketplace or, if you own a vessel, list your boat to reach thousands of travellers. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
